This release introduces **organizations** (multi-tenancy) and fixes four critical vulnerabilities found during a security audit.

> [!WARNING]
> This release contains breaking changes and requires a database migration. Read the "Breaking Changes" section before upgrading, and back up your database first.

## Organizations

Repositories now belong to an **organization** instead of a single isolated user.

- Create organizations with `owner` / `admin` / `member` roles
- Invite teammates **in-app** (the invited user must already have a Nexploy account)
- Member management page: change roles, remove members, cancel pending invitations
- Organization switcher in the sidebar
- Pending invitations surfaced on `/account`
- Guards against removing the last `owner`, and against deleting an organization that still owns repositories

The migration automatically creates a personal organization for every existing user and reassigns their repositories to it. No manual action is required.

## Security

Four critical vulnerabilities fixed:

- **Cross-user isolation** — actions on containers, repositories, builds, environment variables, domains, pipelines and stages now verify membership of the organization owning the resource, not just the caller's role. Previously any `developer` account could act on **any** resource in the instance, including Nexploy's own infrastructure.
- **Container escape** — privileged mode is now rejected server-side, and host bind mounts are restricted by a denylist (`/`, `/etc`, `/root`, `/boot`, `/proc`, `/sys`, `/var/lib/docker`, `/var/run/docker.sock`). A default `PidsLimit` was added.
- **Traefik config injection** — the `host`, `path` and `containerName` fields of a domain are now strictly validated. A backtick previously allowed injecting arbitrary Traefik router rules and hijacking traffic destined for another service.
- **WebSocket terminal** — container ownership is verified before opening a session, and the `user` query parameter (which allowed forcing execution as `root`) is now validated.

## Breaking Changes

- **Database migration required.** `Repository.organizationId` becomes mandatory and is backfilled automatically. The migration is not reversible — back up before upgrading.
- **The `developer` role loses permissions.** `manage` and `remove` on images, networks and volumes are now restricted to `admin`.
- **Privileged containers are no longer supported.** The option was removed from the creation form and is rejected by the API.
- **Sensitive host mounts are rejected.** An existing container bound to a denylisted path will fail to be recreated.
- **Existing domains are revalidated on edit.** A domain containing characters outside the allowed format must be corrected.

## Other Changes

- `server.ts` split into dedicated modules under `src/server/`: configuration, proxies, WebSocket routing, authorization, lifecycle
- Fixed a conflict between the WebSocket proxy and Next.js's internal router that closed terminal connections
- Aligned `@better-auth/*` versions that caused a duplicated `@better-auth/core` install
